title: korthalsella howensis
intro: Korthalsella howensis is a rare and unusual epiphytic plant native to Australia, specifically found in the coastal regions of New South Wales and Queensland. As a member of the Santalaceae family, it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Korthalsella howensis are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it potentially useful in the treatment of various health conditions. However, due to its rarity and limited research, Korthalsella howensis is not commonly used in herbal preparations, and more studies are needed to fully understand its potential medicinal applications.
